# LazyBlog

> A personal blog transmitting thoughts over imperfect radio waves. Expect noise, fading, and low bandwidth—but always authentic signals.

## Posts

- [10X54](https://doantung99.github.io/blog/posts/10x54.md): Reverse an ESP32-S3 firmware crackme, XOR-deobfuscate its strings, and recover the flag by cracking an LCG keystream from just the V1T{ prefix — no password needed.
- [78CT](https://doantung99.github.io/blog/posts/78ct.md): Geolocate a dragon-shaped park from an aerial photo, find the Google Maps review tagged #v1tnamese, then decode the case-and-digit steganography hidden in its lake-themed words.
- [Anime Shop](https://doantung99.github.io/blog/posts/anime-shop.md): PrestaShop deployed from its GitHub source tree leaves dev mode ON, chaining an unauth Symfony profiler file-read to an APP_SECRET-signed /_fragment SSRF that reflects an internal-only flag.
- [B1tsy Ducky](https://doantung99.github.io/blog/posts/b1tsy-ducky.md): A Bitsy game ships a Go-WASM 'duck' that derives an AES-GCM key from three page-local inputs; reconstruct the exact password string offline and decrypt the flag.
- [BootRoot Root of No Return](https://doantung99.github.io/blog/posts/bootroot-root-of-no-return.md): Carve a recovered bootkit installer out of a Windows 2000 disk image, disassemble the 512-byte MBR it writes, and reverse a tiny in-memory SUB-0x0D loop to recover a flag the boot code never prints.
- [China Crack 202](https://doantung99.github.io/blog/posts/china-crack-202.md): A nested password-protected archive whose key was recycled verbatim from last year's 'Tryna Crack' challenge; the flag's hash suffix was the giveaway that closed the loop.
- [Classless](https://doantung99.github.io/blog/posts/classless.md): A stripped C++ object-VM whose success path XOR-decodes the flag from .rodata, so we skip the trilingual type-confusion vault and pull the flag straight out of the binary.
- [Diddy License Checker](https://doantung99.github.io/blog/posts/diddy-license-checker.md): A Linux ELF crackme that derives an AES-128-CBC key and IV from innocent-looking inputs, hides the ciphertext behind a repeating-XOR mask, and double hex-encodes the flag.
- [Duck Nettool Revenge](https://doantung99.github.io/blog/posts/duck-nettool-revenge.md): Command injection through a brutally restrictive character-set filter, solved by globbing binaries with ? wildcards and tricking dash into leaking app.py through its own error messages.
- [Duck Smuzzle](https://doantung99.github.io/blog/posts/duck-smuzzle.md): Chain an X-Forwarded-For allowlist bypass and an OpenAPI operationId leak into an X-Accel-Redirect file read, forge a JWT, then h2c-smuggle past Caddy to reach the blocked /duck route.
- [Duck Wiki](https://doantung99.github.io/blog/posts/duck-wiki.md): Stored XSS in Wiki.js via a Vue scoped-slot destructuring-default SSTI, chained with an incremental ModSecurity CRS bypass to exfiltrate the bot's localStorage flag.
- [Ducks Ping-Pong](https://doantung99.github.io/blog/posts/ducks-ping-pong.md): A user-mode EXE ping-pongs a buffer through a Windows kernel driver, then mixes it locally; emulate both halves offline to recover the flag.
- [Ducks Ping-Pong Revenge](https://doantung99.github.io/blog/posts/ducks-ping-pong-revenge.md): A user-mode/kernel ping-pong where the flag only decrypts after five driver-validated stage commitments rebuild the final key state.
- [Green Goblin](https://doantung99.github.io/blog/posts/green-goblin.md): Carve and reverse a dropper from a Windows 11 RAM dump to find which five OS-internal artifacts hold the real fragments, then ROT47-decode them into the flag.
- [HVL](https://doantung99.github.io/blog/posts/hvl.md): A troll web challenge that hides its real flag in a substitution-cipher font, where emoji codepoints are mapped to flag characters and rendered on screen.
- [Kaspersky](https://doantung99.github.io/blog/posts/kaspersky.md): A 5-question Kaspersky OSINT quiz solved by an LLM grinding artifacts while I steered the search, killed a costly false trail, and forced the final answer into native Cyrillic.
- [New Way to store my CP](https://doantung99.github.io/blog/posts/new-way-to-store-my-cp.md): A two-stage stego chain: zero-width Unicode in a Pastebin hid a StegCloak password, and a YouTube video was actually a yt-media-storage packet container that decrypted to a Quack-padded flag.
- [Quack CIA](https://doantung99.github.io/blog/posts/quack-cia.md): An OSINT trail from a video to a GitHub repo, ending in a Vim undo file whose Base64-encoded flag survived being deleted from the working copy.
- [Scary Duck](https://doantung99.github.io/blog/posts/scary-duck.md): An MP4 with an appended password-protected ZIP whose password is split across a 1-bit image block and an 8-tone audio sequence, with the flag hidden behind XOR + reverse + Base62.
- [ShahInRev](https://doantung99.github.io/blog/posts/shahinrev.md): Reversing a stripped, anti-debugged ELF keygen-style checker by reconstructing its byte-VM in Python and recovering the 8-byte flag body that drives a hardcoded accumulator.
- [Slop Anti or Anti Slop](https://doantung99.github.io/blog/posts/slop-anti-or-anti-slop.md): A deliberately noisy crypto challenge where the printed values are decoys; the real AES-GCM key is rebuilt from a Decimal polynomial, a modular affine map plus Lagrange interpolation, and a 70-million-round repeated-squaring chain.
- [StaleMate](https://doantung99.github.io/blog/posts/stalemate.md): A userland reimplementation of the io_uring provided-buffer-ring bug (CVE-2024-0582\): unregister leaves the mmap alive, so a stale mapping over a reused slot gives a UAF write to forge a page-table entry onto the cred page.
- [StaleMate Revenge](https://doantung99.github.io/blog/posts/stalemate-revenge.md): A hardened userland io_uring/PBUF use-after-free where deterministic heap grooming leaks the workspace MAC keys, lets me forge a self-referential page table, and rewrite the sealed record chain into a winning position.
- [XTS-AES](https://doantung99.github.io/blog/posts/xts-aes.md): Reverse a leaked ESP32-S3 provisioning firmware to re-derive a read-protected flash-encryption key from readable device data, then decrypt the XTS-AES flash dump to recover the flag.
- [try](https://doantung99.github.io/blog/posts/try.md): A Windows PE64 'sealed input verifier' buried under fake packer sections, solved by emulating a tiny constraint VM and matching a 64-bit hash — the LLM ground through the disassembly, I steered it off the unpacking trap.

## Tags

- crypto: https://doantung99.github.io/blog/tags/crypto
- ctf: https://doantung99.github.io/blog/tags/ctf

- hardware: https://doantung99.github.io/blog/tags/hardware
- misc: https://doantung99.github.io/blog/tags/misc
- osint: https://doantung99.github.io/blog/tags/osint
- pwn: https://doantung99.github.io/blog/tags/pwn
- rev: https://doantung99.github.io/blog/tags/rev
- v1t-ctf-2026: https://doantung99.github.io/blog/tags/v1t-ctf-2026
